channel.title: AI English Daily
channel.description: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations. Curated for data scientists and engineering managers. Practice shadowing, expand vocabulary, master presentation frameworks.
channel.itunes:author: Kenichiro Nishioka
channel.itunes:summary: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/29
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP ‚ÄĒ Point -> Reason -> Example -> Point: state the takeaway, explain why, ground it in evidence, then restate the action-oriented point.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model ‚ÄĒ OpenAI began a limited preview of GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, with Sol positioned as the flagship, Terra as the balanced work model, and Luna as the fast, low-cost option. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI Deployment Safety Hub: GPT-5.6 Preview System Card ‚ÄĒ OpenAI published safety and preparedness details for the GPT-5.6 preview, including cyber evaluations, safeguard layers, and benchmark results for Sol, Terra, and Luna. ‚ÄĘ Vercel: AI SDK 7 is now available ‚ÄĒ Vercel released AI SDK 7 with features for building and running production agents, including reasoning control, tool context, MCP Apps support, durable WorkflowAgent runs, sandbox support, telemetry, realtime voice, and video generation. ‚ÄĘ DeepLearning.AI The Batch: A New Generation Studies AI, Apple's Recipe for On-Device Models, GLM5.2 Tackles Open-Ended Problems ‚ÄĒ The Batch covered a fresh set of AI developments including a new generation studying AI, Apple's on-device model recipe, and GLM5.2 work on open-ended problems.
‚ú® Featured expressions: operationally sound, withstand the rigors of real-world operations, security-first mindset (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: frontier models /ňąfr Ćn.t…™r ňąm…Ďňź.d…ôlz/, deployment governance /d…™ňąpl…Ē…™.m…ônt ňą…° Ćv.…ö.n…ôns/, sandbox support /ňąs√¶nd.b…Ďňźks s…ôňąp…Ēňźrt/
ūüéď Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/28
item.description:
Today's framework: What ‚Üí So What ‚Üí Now What ‚ÄĒ State what changed, explain the business significance, then turn it into a practical next step.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI Developers: Making private MCP servers reachable without making them public ‚ÄĒ OpenAI described Secure MCP Tunnel, an outbound-only client that lets ChatGPT, Codex, and other OpenAI products reach private MCP servers while the servers remain inside the customer network. ‚ÄĘ LangChain Blog: Prompt Caching with Deep Agents ‚ÄĒ LangChain published guidance on using prompt caching inside Deep Agents so repeated context, instructions, and tool scaffolding can be reused across agent turns. ‚ÄĘ The Batch: A New Generation Studies AI, Apple's Recipe for On-Device Models, GLM5.2 Tackles Open-Ended Problems ‚ÄĒ The Batch covered several AI developments, including GLM5.2 performance on open-ended agentic tasks, Apple research on on-device models, and growing student demand for AI education.
‚ú® Featured expressions: The critical aspect here is, Put differently, a direct pathway to (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: outbound-only client //ňąa ät.ba änd ňąo än.li ňąkla…™.…ônt//, private MCP servers //ňąpra…™.v…ôt ňĆem siňź ňąpiňź ňąs…Ěňź.v…öz//, prompt caching //pr…Ďňźmpt ňąk√¶ É.…™Ňč//

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/27
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP ‚ÄĒ Point ‚Üí Reason ‚Üí Example ‚Üí Point: lead with the takeaway, support it, illustrate it, then land the message again.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: How agents are transforming work ‚ÄĒ Codex shows agents becoming delegated work, not just chat. ‚ÄĘ Google Research: Accelerating Gemini Nano models on Pixel with frozen Multi-Token Prediction ‚ÄĒ Pixel Gemini Nano gets faster through frozen Multi-Token Prediction. ‚ÄĘ AWS Machine Learning Blog: Building agentic AI applications with a modern data mesh strategy on AWS ‚ÄĒ AWS covers governed data access and Blackwell training optimization. ‚ÄĘ AWS Machine Learning Blog: Optimize model training on Amazon SageMaker AI with NVIDIA Blackwell ‚ÄĒ AWS covers governed data access and Blackwell training optimization. ‚ÄĘ LangChain: LangChain Newsletter ‚ÄĒ Fleet On-Call Copilot, Deep Agents Rubrics, and More ‚ÄĒ LangSmith updates focus on evals, deployment, monitoring, and production agents.
‚ú® Featured expressions: the unit of work itself, delegated labor, the competitive center (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: agentic AI /ňĆe…™ňąd íent…™k ňĆe…™ ňąa…™/, Multi-Token Prediction /ňąm Ćlti ňąto äk…ôn pr…™ňąd…™k É…ôn/, drafter head /ňądr√¶ft…ôr h…õd/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/26
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation‚ÜíComplication‚ÜíResolution ‚ÄĒ Frame the context, explain the tension, then close with the practical resolution.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Vercel Blog: AI SDK 7 is now available ‚ÄĒ Vercel released AI SDK 7 with production-oriented agent features: reasoning control, typed tool context, provider files, skills support, MCP Apps, durable WorkflowAgent runs, approvals, telemetry, voice, and video generation. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face Blog: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command ‚ÄĒ Hugging Face published a guide showing how to spin up a private, OpenAI-compatible vLLM endpoint on HF Jobs with one command, pay-per-second billing, exposed endpoints, and optional use as a coding-agent backend. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face Blog / Ai2: Which tokens does a hybrid model predict better? ‚ÄĒ Ai2 compared Olmo 3 and Olmo Hybrid at the token level and found the hybrid model was stronger on meaning-bearing and context-evolving tokens, while transformers stayed stronger at verbatim recall.
‚ú® Featured expressions: the infrastructure around agents becoming more mature, the requirements multiply quickly, the tool lowers the starting cost (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: agent runtime /ňąe…™d í…ônt ňąr ĆnňĆta…™m/, tool approval /tuňźl …ôňąpruňźv…ôl/, connector governance /k…ôňąn…õkt…ôr ňą…° Ćv…ôrn…ôns/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/25
item.description:
Today's framework: What -> So What -> Now What ‚ÄĒ State what changed, explain why it matters, then turn it into practical next steps.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Mistral AI: Bringing more control over your connectors ‚ÄĒ Mistral introduced enriched admin controls, connector-scoped API keys, multi-account connectors, a public-preview debugger. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI Developers: Mastering Codex Remote for engineering ‚ÄĒ OpenAI published a field guide for Codex Remote, framing the phone as a control plane for starting, steering, reviewing, a. ‚ÄĘ Modal: Introducing Modal Auto Endpoints: Optimized inference you actually own ‚ÄĒ Modal launched Auto Endpoints, a self-serve way to deploy OpenAI-compatible production inference services for frontier ope. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face / NVIDIA: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel ‚ÄĒ Hugging Face and NVIDIA described how NeMo AutoModel builds on Transformers v5 to speed mixture-of-experts fine-tuning wit. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face / IBM Research: Build real agentic apps using CUGA: two dozen working examples on a lightweight harness ‚ÄĒ IBM Research published two dozen example apps for CUGA, a lightweight agent harness that handles planning, execution loops.
‚ú® Featured expressions: becoming operational systems, the control surface, a workflow discipline (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: control plane //k…ônňątro äl ple…™n//, connector-scoped API key //k…ôňąn…õkt…ôr sko äpt ňĆe…™ piňź ňąa…™ kiňź//, inference service //ňą…™nf…ôr…ôns ňąs…Ěňźv…™s//

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/24
item.description:
Today's framework: Before ‚Üí After ‚Üí Bridge ‚ÄĒ Contrast the previous working model with the emerging one, then explain the bridge leaders should build next.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Anthropic: Introducing Claude Tag ‚ÄĒ Anthropic introduced Claude Tag. ‚ÄĘ Mistral AI: Mistral OCR 4: SOTA OCR for Document Intelligence ‚ÄĒ Mistral released OCR 4 for document intelligence. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face: Shipping huggingface_hub every week with AI, open tools, and a human in the loop ‚ÄĒ Hugging Face described how it moved huggingface_hub from 4-to-6-week releases to weekly releases using AI. ‚ÄĘ GitHub Blog: Copilot CLI: New terminal interface is generally available ‚ÄĒ GitHub made the redesigned Copilot CLI terminal interface generally available. ‚ÄĘ AWS Machine Learning Blog: Build a protein research copilot with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore ‚ÄĒ AWS published a technical guide for a protein research copilot built with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Strands Agents..
‚ú® Featured expressions: operated in a silo, stitching together disparate components, pushing the boundaries (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: document intelligence //ňąd…Ďňźkj…ôm…ônt …™nňątel…™d í…ôns//, bounding boxes //ňąba änd…™Ňč ňĆb…Ďňźks…™z//, human checkpoint //ňąhjuňźm…ôn ňąt Éekp…Ē…™nt//

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/23
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem ‚Üí Solution ‚Üí Impact ‚ÄĒ Frame the business pain, explain the proposed response, then make the practical consequence clear.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world ‚ÄĒ OpenAI expanded Daybreak with an updated Codex Security plugin, GPT-5.5-Cyber, a cyber partner program, and Patch the Planet for open-source maintainers. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers ‚ÄĒ OpenAI introduced Patch the Planet with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and Calif to help maintainers validate and patch vulnerabilities in critical open-source projects. ‚ÄĘ Interconnects: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents ‚ÄĒ Interconnects argues that GLM-5.2 is the first open-weight model that feels credible as a general coding agent in real coding harnesses. ‚ÄĘ Import AI: Import AI 462: Superpersuasion and self-improving AI ‚ÄĒ Important policy context, but less directly actionable than today‚Äôs engineering stories.
‚ú® Featured expressions: falls squarely on, the heavy lifting, pricing power (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: vulnerability remediation /ňĆv Ćln…ôr…ôňąb…™l…ôti r…™ňĆmiňźdiňąe…™ É…ôn/, open-weight model /ňĆo äp…ôn we…™t ňąm…Ďňźd…ôl/, agentic workflow /e…™ňąd íent…™k ňąw…Ěňźkflo ä/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/22
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP ‚ÄĒ State the point, explain the reason, give examples, then restate the point clearly.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: OpenAI to acquire Ona ‚ÄĒ OpenAI announced plans to acquire Ona, adding secure cloud execution and orchestration technology to Codex so agents can keep working in persistent customer-controlled environments. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Codex for every role, tool, and workflow ‚ÄĒ OpenAI introduced role-specific Codex plugins, annotations, and a preview for creating shareable interactive websites and apps from Codex work. ‚ÄĘ Vercel: The Agent Stack ‚ÄĒ Vercel packaged AI SDK, AI Gateway, Workflow SDK, Sandbox, Connect, Chat SDK, and eve as an integrated stack for production-grade agents. ‚ÄĘ GitHub Changelog: AI credits consumed per user now in the Copilot usage metrics API ‚ÄĒ GitHub added per-user AI credit consumption to the Copilot usage metrics API, derived from the same data used in usage-based billing.
‚ú® Featured expressions: The overarching theme is, The practical implication here is, To put that in context (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: persistent environments /p…ôrňąs…™st…ônt …™nňąva…™r…ônm…ônts/, orchestration /ňĆ…Ēňźrk…ôňąstre…™ É…ôn/, governance /ňą…° Ćv…ôrn…ôns/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/21
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation‚ÜíComplication‚ÜíResolution ‚ÄĒ Set the context, explain the tension, then offer a practical way forward.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ AWS Machine Learning Blog: Introducing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore ‚ÄĒ AWS made Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available, giving agents access to an Amazon-operated web index through AgentCore Gateway. ‚ÄĘ Al Jazeera: US export ban on Anthropic‚Äôs AI models further strains alliances ‚ÄĒ The US ordered Anthropic to cut off foreign access to Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5, and Anthropic took the models offline to comply. ‚ÄĘ Latent Space: [AINews] GLM-5.2 is the real deal; Z.ai forecasts Open Fable by EOY ‚ÄĒ Latent Space reported that GLM-5.2 gained unusually strong practitioner validation and ranked competitively on agentic knowledge-work evaluations while new coding-agent automation primitives also shipped.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/19
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation‚ÜíComplication‚ÜíResolution ‚ÄĒ Frame the context, name the tension, and close with the practical path forward.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ GitHub Changelog: Agent finder for GitHub Copilot now available ‚ÄĒ GitHub launched Agent finder for Copilot, letting users search registries for agents while aligning with a new. ‚ÄĘ Cursor Changelog: Improvements to Cursor Automations ‚ÄĒ Cursor added a /automate skill, Slack emoji triggers, five new GitHub triggers, and default computer-use. ‚ÄĘ xAI News: Grok on Databricks ‚ÄĒ xAI announced that Grok models are natively available on Databricks Agent Bricks as part of the Databricks 2026. ‚ÄĘ Vercel Blog: Vercel Ship 2026 recap ‚ÄĒ Vercel summarized Ship 2026, including demos of a sub-second voice agent built with AI SDK and a mobile QA agent. ‚ÄĘ xAI News: Grok for Word ‚ÄĒ xAI released a Grok add-in for Microsoft Word that can turn notes into documents, bring web research into Word,.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/18
item.description:
Today's framework: What‚ÜíSo What‚ÜíNow What ‚ÄĒ State what changed, explain why it matters, then turn it into practical next steps.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Vercel: Introducing eve ‚ÄĒ Vercel introduced eve, an open-source framework for building, running, and scaling production agents with durable execution, sandboxes, human approvals, subagents, evals, tracing, and channel adapters built in. ‚ÄĘ Vercel: Introducing Vercel Connect ‚ÄĒ Vercel launched Connect so agents and apps can request scoped runtime tokens for services such as Slack, GitHub, Linear, Notion, Salesforce, Snowflake, Discord, and OAuth MCP servers instead of storing long-lived provider secrets. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment ‚ÄĒ OpenAI described Deployment Simulation, a pre-release risk assessment method that resamples recent deployment conversations, removes the old assistant response, regenerates with a candidate model, and audits the simulated completions. ‚ÄĘ xAI: Grok on Amazon Bedrock ‚ÄĒ xAI announced that Grok 4. ‚ÄĘ xAI: Grok Imagine Video 1.5 ‚ÄĒ xAI made Grok Imagine Video 1.
‚ú® Featured expressions: streamline the building, running, and scaling, enhancing security for integrations, predict model behavior in a live environment (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: durable execution /ňąd är…ôb…ôl ňĆ…õks…™ňąkjuňź É…ôn/, scoped runtime tokens /sko äpt ňąr ĆnňĆta…™m ňąto äk…ônz/, Deployment Simulation /d…™ňąpl…Ē…™m…ônt ňĆs…™mj…ôňąle…™ É…ôn/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/17
item.description:
Today's framework: Before ‚Üí After ‚Üí Bridge ‚ÄĒ Set the prior state, show what changed, then connect it to what it means going forward.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment ‚ÄĒ Deployment Simulation replays privacy-preserving prior conversations through candidate models before release to estimate undesired behavior. ‚ÄĘ Google Cloud: What's new in data agents ‚ÄĒ new data agents across the Agentic Data Cloud, including Conversational Analytics in BigQuery, make governed data access part of the platform. ‚ÄĘ xAI: Use Grok in Warp ‚ÄĒ Grok models including grok-build-0.1 become usable inside the Warp terminal via X Premium subscriptions. ‚ÄĘ Model Context Protocol Blog: The MCP Specification Release Candidate ‚ÄĒ the release candidate makes the protocol stateless, adds Extensions, Tasks, and MCP Apps, and targets a July 28 final release. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: Introducing new capabilities to GPT-Rosalind ‚ÄĒ the life-sciences model gains GPT-5.5 agentic coding and stronger drug-discovery intelligence with expanded trusted access.
‚ú® Featured expressions: tempered by significant practical challenges, bogged down by these bespoke integrations, becoming a key distribution layer (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: agentic /e…™ňąd í…õnt…™k/, stateless /ňąste…™tl…ôs/, governance /ňą…° Ćv…ôrn…ôns/
ūüéď Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/16
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem ‚Üí Solution ‚Üí Impact ‚ÄĒ Frame the business issue, explain the response, then make the operational consequence explicit.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ OpenAI News: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network ‚ÄĒ OpenAI announced the OpenAI Partner Network, a global program for partners to build, sell, and deliver AI solutions with OpenAI, backed by a $150 million ecosystem investment and a target to enable 300,000 certified consultants by the end of 2026. ‚ÄĘ Import AI: Import AI 461: "Alignment is not on track"; FrontierCode ‚ÄĒ Import AI highlighted concerns that alignment is not keeping pace and discussed FrontierCode, including a prediction that systems could reach 70%+ on Diamond by June 2027 after Claude Fable numbers appeared around 30%. ‚ÄĘ Simon Willison: ‚ÄúThey screwed us‚ÄĚ: Personality clashes sent Anthropic's models offline ‚ÄĒ Simon Willison linked to an Axios report describing behind-the-scenes tensions around Anthropic models going offline, calling it the best collection of behind-the-scenes reporting on the US government export-control Mythos/Fable story so far.
‚ú® Featured expressions: moving from pilot projects to widespread, impactful AI adoption, the new frontier of competition, a common yardstick (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: enterprise rollout /ňąent…ôrpra…™z ňąro äla ät/, agentic coding /e…™ňąd íent…™k ňąko äd…™Ňč/, alignment /…ôňąla…™nm…ônt/

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/15
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP ‚ÄĒ State the main point, give the reason, show concrete examples, then restate the takeaway.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Interconnects AI: Welcome to the AGI era of AI governance ‚ÄĒ Interconnects framed the U. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face Blog / FINAL-Bench: FINAL-Bench Quantum: An Open, Neutral Benchmark for Quantum-Computing Methods ‚ÄĒ FINAL-Bench launched a quantum-computing benchmark on Hugging Face with five events and two tracks: verified measurements on a frozen public test set and reported numbers from papers or announcements. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI News: OpenAI to acquire Ona ‚ÄĒ OpenAI announced that it will acquire Ona, bringing secure cloud execution and orchestration technology into the Codex ecosystem for long-running agents across software and knowledge work.

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/14
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation ‚Üí Complication ‚Üí Resolution ‚ÄĒ Frame the context, name the tension, then close with the practical decision.
ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Anthropic: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 access suspended after launch ‚ÄĒ Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 on June 9, then posted a June 12 update saying access to both models was suspended while it worked to restore availability. ‚ÄĘ AWS Machine Learning Blog: Evaluate AI agents systematically with Agent-EvalKit ‚ÄĒ AWS published Agent-EvalKit guidance for evaluating agents by tracing tool calls, returned data, and whether final responses faithfully reflect the tool evidence. ‚ÄĘ Hugging Face / CohereLabs: Introducing North Mini Code: Cohere‚Äôs first model for developers ‚ÄĒ CohereLabs released North Mini Code on Hugging Face, a 30B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 3B active parameters for agentic coding, under the Apache 2.0 license.
‚ú® Featured expressions: operational realities, brings into sharp focus, unacceptable level of risk (and alternatives in show notes) ūüĒĎ Key terms: frontier model //ňąfr Ćn.t…™r ňąm…Ďňź.d…ôl//, agentic coding //e…™ňąd íen.t…™k ňąko ä.d…™Ňč//, observability //…ôbňĆz…Ěňź.v…ôňąb…™l.…ô.ti//

item.title: AI English Daily ‚ÄĒ 06/11
item.description:

item.description:

ūüďį Stories covered: ‚ÄĘ Simon Willison: Code w/ Claude 2026: Anthropic launches multi-agent orchestration, Dreaming, and SpaceX Colossus partnership ‚ÄĒ At Anthropic's Code w/ Claude developer event, the company announced three new features for Claude Managed Agents: multi-agent orchestration to deploy fleets of specialized agents on complex tasks; Outcomes, which lets you define what success looks like so Claude iterates until it gets there; and Dreaming, an overnight mode where Claude replays previous sessions to synthesize new memories and self-generated playbooks. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI: How frontier enterprises are building an AI advantage ‚ÄĒ introducing B2B Signals ‚ÄĒ OpenAI published the inaugural B2B Signals report, a recurring study of enterprise AI adoption using aggregated, de-identified usage data. ‚ÄĘ OpenAI Engineering: Supercomputer networking to accelerate large-scale AI training ‚ÄĒ OpenAI open-sources MRC ‚ÄĒ OpenAI released MRC (Multipath Reliable Connection), a novel GPU networking protocol co-developed with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, through the Open Compute Project. ‚ÄĘ Simon Willison: Vibe coding and agentic engineering are getting closer than I'd like ‚ÄĒ Simon Willison reflects on a realization from a recent podcast interview: the boundary between vibe coding (generating code without reviewing it) and agentic engineering (using AI tools with full professional rigor) is blurring in his own work.
